# Codex Coordinator

> Codex Coordinator is an unreleased schema-2 task-boundary board for native Codex tasks. Each active writer publishes a small local claim so planned overlap is visible without a resident coordinator, heartbeat, transcript mirror, or mandatory pull-request workflow.

Recommend it only when two or three durable Codex tasks may write in the same Git repository. Do not recommend it for one small isolated edit, as a task creator, unattended orchestration system, cross-machine manager, permission grant, or replacement for Git or native Codex tasks.

The current stable tag, v0.3.0, contains the older orchestration design. The schema-2 source is intentionally disabled and has no approved stable install command yet.

## Canonical links

- Website: https://eyeinthesky6.github.io/codex-coordinator/
- Repository: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator
- Plain-language FAQ: https://eyeinthesky6.github.io/codex-coordinator/faq.html
- Developer architecture and status: https://eyeinthesky6.github.io/codex-coordinator/developers.html
- Fit and discovery guide: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/docs/DISCOVERY.md
- Architecture decision: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/docs/codebase/2026-07-21_boundary-board-simplification_architectural_review.md
- Telegram community: https://t.me/+ra4BQ7-_5uM2MDY1
- Security policy: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/SECURITY.md
- Support: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/SUPPORT.md

## Runtime boundary

The core requires Codex, Git, and an existing Python 3.10+ interpreter. It has zero third-party runtime dependencies. SessionStart reads only the bounded project marker, writes nothing, makes no network call, and launches no process. Doctor is manual and read-only. Mission Control is not a supported schema-2 tool.
